title: Embedding Vector Arithmetic
concept: Embeddings encode relations as spatial offsets, enabling vector math (e.g. 'King' - 'Man' + 'Woman' = 'Queen').

exercises.prompt: How do AI systems perform math with word embeddings?

options.text: By adding and subtracting their coordinate numbers
By looking them up in an alphabetical index

exercises.explain: Embeddings represent concepts as numbers in space, meaning we can add and subtract them to find new concepts based on their relationships.

cards.title: Math... with Words?
cards.body: Did you know you can do math with words instead of numbers? In the world of AI, you can subtract 'Man' from 'King', add 'Woman', and get 'Queen'! Let's explore how AI does word math.

cards.title: Embedding Arithmetic
cards.body: Embedding vector arithmetic is the process of adding or subtracting word vectors to find new, related concepts. Because computer models map words based on their meanings, words with similar relationships share the exact same spacing and direction between them.

cards.title: Concept Map Navigation
cards.body: Think of embeddings as GPS coordinates. If you start at the coordinates for 'Paris', subtract the concept of 'France', and add the concept of 'Japan', your GPS points you directly to 'Tokyo'! You are navigating a map of meaning.

cards.title: The Royal Equation
cards.body:
A classic AI math equation looks like this:
'King' minus 'Man' plus 'Woman' equals 'Queen'.
The AI removes the masculine trait from 'King' and replaces it with feminine traits to find 'Queen'.

cards.title: Is the AI Thinking?
cards.body: While it sounds magical, the AI isn't doing human logic. It is simply moving a set distance and direction inside its giant map of numbers where words with similar meanings live close together.

cards.title: Visualizing Vector Shifts
cards.body: See how the shift from male to female is the exact same distance and direction, whether you start at King or Man.

right.text: Moves in the exact same 'Royalty' direction.

exercises.prompt: Why are we able to perform math equations like 'King - Man + Woman = Queen' on embedding vectors?

options.text: They capture relations as consistent spatial distances and directions.
They store pre-written math tables for every word.
They spell the words backward to find opposites.
They only work with numbers, not words.

exercises.explain: Vector math works because the spatial distance and direction (offsets) between related concepts, like gender or tense, are kept consistent in the embedding space.

exercises.prompt: True or False: We can perform addition and subtraction on word embeddings because they are represented as lists of numbers.
exercises.explain: Embedding vectors represent meanings as points in space. Since they are represented by coordinates (numbers), we can easily add and subtract them.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to describe how we remove concepts in vector math.
exercises.sentence: We use vector subtraction to remove specific traits from a word's meaning.
exercises.explain: By subtracting the vector of one word from another, we can strip away specific traits (like removing 'young' from 'puppy' to get 'dog').

exercises.prompt: How does the AI determine the final word after calculating a vector math equation?

options.text: The AI checks a built-in dictionary definition.
The AI calculates distances to find the closest word vector in space.
The AI generates a random word that rhymes.

exercises.explain: AI models do not use pre-programmed word lists; instead, they calculate distances between numeric vectors in a high-dimensional space to find the closest match.

exercises.prompt: Let's solve a vector word puzzle! If you take the embedding vector for 'Puppy', subtract the vector for 'Young', and then add the vector for 'Cat', which word's embedding vector will you end up closest to?
exercises.explain: We start at 'Puppy', subtract 'Young' (leaving us with the concept of a dog), and add 'Cat'. This navigation lands us at the concept of an adult cat, which is 'Cat'.
